transcript.speaker: Bellwether
transcript.text: It is late autumn in Bellwether: damp, windy and changeable with misty mornings.

transcript.speaker: Narrator
transcript.text: The bus leaves you at Bellwether just after nine. Its engine noise thins along the road until the village is quiet again.

transcript.speaker: Narrator
transcript.text: For now, there is the road ahead, your suitcase at your feet, and Eleanor's key in your pocket.

transcript.speaker: Narrator
transcript.text: You wait beneath the shelter. Traffic is sparse enough that every engine can be heard before it appears.

transcript.speaker: Narrator
transcript.text: You stop for a while and listen. Bellwether is quiet, but never entirely still.

transcript.speaker: Narrator
transcript.text: You wait beneath the shelter. Traffic is sparse enough that every engine can be heard before it appears.

transcript.speaker: Observation
transcript.text: The road beyond the bus stop has begun to feel less like the way out and more like one edge of Bellwether.

transcript.speaker: Narrator
transcript.text: You slow down and pay attention to the place rather than merely passing through it.

transcript.speaker: Notebook
transcript.text: The road has a rhythm: Long quiet gaps separate the traffic; regular vehicles are easy to distinguish from unfamiliar ones.

transcript.speaker: Narrator
transcript.text: You make your way to Village Road.

transcript.speaker: Bellwether
transcript.text: The road is busy with passing traffic.

transcript.speaker: Narrator
transcript.text: You pick up a few ordinary household necessities. It is a small thing, but carrying them back makes your stay feel less temporary.

transcript.speaker: Bellwether
transcript.text: Route 7 bus is nearby, approaching Bellwether on the return service.

transcript.speaker: Narrator
transcript.text: Stone cottages lean toward the narrow road. Shop windows, garden gates and the turn toward the church give the village its daily rhythm.

transcript.speaker: Bellwether
transcript.text: Bellwether is settling into its morning rhythm. The road is quiet between scheduled buses. A robin patrols the hedges while rooks call beyond the church.

transcript.speaker: Bellwether
transcript.text: The road has settled into a quieter interval.

transcript.speaker: Narrator
transcript.text: You make your way to Village Green.

transcript.speaker: Bellwether
transcript.text: There are 2 familiar villagers moving around the green.

transcript.speaker: Jonah
transcript.text: You must be Eleanor's relation. I had a loaf riding on whether you'd take the morning bus.

transcript.speaker: You
transcript.text: People talk about strangers here, then?

transcript.speaker: Jonah
transcript.text: Only until they stop being strangers. Around here, that rarely takes long.

transcript.speaker: Jonah
transcript.text: Here. Bread. Consider it an apology from the village gossip network.

transcript.speaker: Journal
transcript.text: Side Story completed: A Warm Welcome.

transcript.label: Sit on the Bench and Watch the Green

transcript.speaker: Narrator
transcript.text: You sit beneath the old oak. People cross the green on errands that make sense to them and, slowly, begin to make sense to you.

transcript.speaker: Bellwether
transcript.text: The weather has shifted: heavy rain, 9°C, with brisk wind.

transcript.speaker: Bellwether
transcript.text: Jonah is taking a short break on the village green.

transcript.speaker: Narrator
transcript.text: You slow down and pay attention to the place rather than merely passing through it.

transcript.speaker: Notebook
transcript.text: The green and the bell: From one side of the oak the church is hidden, but its bell carries across the whole green with surprising clarity.

transcript.speaker: Notebook
transcript.text: Recent activity at Village Green: There are 1 familiar villager moving around the green.

transcript.label: Follow the path to the churchyard

transcript.speaker: Narrator
transcript.text: You make your way to St Bartholomew's Churchyard.

transcript.speaker: Narrator
transcript.text: The yews keep the air still. Beyond the wall, Bellwether continues with errands, doors and distant engines.

transcript.speaker: Narrator
transcript.text: You linger without a destination and watch Bellwether carry on around you.

transcript.speaker: Bellwether
transcript.text: The village has grown busier around the green and shops.

transcript.speaker: Narrator
transcript.text: You make your way to Village Road.

transcript.speaker: Bellwether
transcript.text: The road has settled into a quieter interval.

transcript.speaker: You
transcript.text: How has your day been?

transcript.speaker: Mrs Ellis
transcript.text: Forgive me, dear, my mind wandered. Go on.

transcript.speaker: You
transcript.text: Good to see you. Take care.

transcript.speaker: Mrs Ellis
transcript.text: Goodbye, dear. Mind how you go.

transcript.speaker: Narrator
transcript.text: You walk without a destination. Familiar doors and hedges begin to separate themselves from the blur of arrival.

transcript.speaker: Bellwether
transcript.text: Mara is having a brief chat with a neighbour.

transcript.speaker: Bellwether
transcript.text: Mrs Ellis is continuing her ordinary morning round.

transcript.speaker: Bellwether
transcript.text: Route 7 bus is nearby, waiting briefly to keep to the timetable.

transcript.speaker: Observation
transcript.text: You can now place several houses by their gates, gardens, and the sounds that come from behind them.

transcript.speaker: Notebook
transcript.text: You review the last few things you thought worth remembering.

transcript.speaker: Notebook
transcript.text: The road has a rhythm — Long quiet gaps separate the traffic; regular vehicles are easy to distinguish from unfamiliar ones.

transcript.speaker: Notebook
transcript.text: The green and the bell — From one side of the oak the church is hidden, but its bell carries across the whole green with surprising clarity.

transcript.speaker: Notebook
transcript.text: Recent activity at Village Green — There are 1 familiar villager moving around the green.

transcript.speaker: Narrator
transcript.text: You make your way to Village Shop.

transcript.speaker: Narrator
transcript.text: You read the local pages over twice. Parish notices and small advertisements reveal more about the surrounding villages than the headlines do.

transcript.speaker: Bellwether
transcript.text: The weather has shifted: light rain, 10°C, with gentle wind.

transcript.speaker: Narrator
transcript.text: You linger without a destination and watch Bellwether carry on around you.

transcript.text: The village has grown busier around the green and shops.

transcript.text: There are 2 familiar villagers moving around the green.

transcript.speaker: You
transcript.text: Morning. How are you?

transcript.speaker: Mrs Ellis
transcript.text: Hello, dear. How are you getting on?

transcript.speaker: You
transcript.text: How has your day been?

transcript.speaker: Mrs Ellis
transcript.text: Forgive me, dear, my mind wandered. Go on.

transcript.speaker: Nearby
transcript.text: You catch part of a conversation.

transcript.speaker: Mara
transcript.text: Morning. You're out early.

transcript.speaker: Mrs Ellis
transcript.text: So are you. I thought I'd take the air while it's quiet.

transcript.speaker: Mara
transcript.text: It won't stay quiet for long.

transcript.speaker: Mrs Ellis
transcript.text: No. It never seems to, once everyone gets moving.

transcript.speaker: Bellwether
transcript.text: Mara is taking a quiet walk across the village green.

transcript.speaker: Bellwether
transcript.text: Mrs Ellis is walking at an unhurried pace across the green.

transcript.text: Route 7 bus is nearby, passing the road beside the village green.

transcript.speaker: Narrator
transcript.text: You make your way to Ashcroft Cottage.

transcript.speaker: Bellwether
transcript.text: Freshly disturbed soil and a growing cleared patch show that someone has been working in the garden.

transcript.speaker: Journal
transcript.text: Main Story completed: A House Left Waiting.

transcript.speaker: Journal
transcript.text: Main Story added: In Eleanor's Hand.

transcript.speaker: Narrator
transcript.text: You choose a book from Eleanor's shelves and read until the sounds outside become part of the room.

transcript.speaker: Narrator
transcript.text: Ivy frames the small windows. Inside, dust hangs in shafts of light and the rooms smell of lavender, books, and a house kept closed too long.

transcript.speaker: Bellwether
transcript.text: The day's movement is beginning to turn homeward. A delivery van passes through the village. Blackbirds work through the wet grass.

transcript.text: Freshly disturbed soil and a growing cleared patch show that someone has been working in the garden.

transcript.text: Mara is checking the cottage garden boundary and hedges.

transcript.label: Watch the Water for a While

transcript.speaker: Narrator
transcript.text: The river looks slow until you fix your attention on leaves and foam moving beneath the footbridge.

transcript.speaker: Narrator
transcript.text: You linger without a destination and watch Bellwether carry on around you.

transcript.label: Take a Slow Walk Around the Green

transcript.speaker: Narrator
transcript.text: You follow the worn paths around the green, learning which gate sticks and where the ground stays damp.

transcript.speaker: Bellwether
transcript.text: Route 7 bus is nearby, passing the road beside the village green.

transcript.speaker: Observation
transcript.text: The green no longer feels like a landmark on a map. You are beginning to recognize its rhythms.

transcript.speaker: Narrator
transcript.text: You read the notices instead of merely glancing at them: a jumble sale, a lost glove, choir practice, someone offering ladder repairs.

transcript.speaker: Observation
transcript.text: A few surnames recur across unrelated notices. Bellwether's clubs, errands, and favours seem to run through the same small circle of households.

transcript.text: Paths across the green: The palest tracks do not simply cross the grass; they connect the bench, noticeboard, road and cottage path in a practiced circuit.
